title: Jet Balance
forecastMethod: Monthly balance anchored to a 3-year seasonal average from 2023-2025; weekly EIA path reconciled to monthly values for 22 weeks after the latest actual week.

lazyChunks.name: weekly
lazyChunks.reason: Weekly regional balances and movement flows stay out of the initial workbook payload.

lazyChunks.name: crudeWeekly
lazyChunks.reason: Weekly crude-runs rows load only when the Crude runs sheet switches to weekly.

lazyChunks.name: reference
lazyChunks.reason: Source inventory, refinery capacity, Kpler metadata, and checksums load only for Reference, Outages, or Crude runs.

guardrails.label: Lazy runtime split
guardrails.detail: 86.9% of generated rows are deferred into lazy chunks.

guardrails.label: Shared crude context
guardrails.detail: 5,344 crude rows are generated once and reused across product workbooks.

guardrails.label: Reference isolation
guardrails.detail: 148.9 KB of reference payload is isolated from initial load.

recommendations.label: Run both-product traces after rebuilds
recommendations.detail: Use the dashboard performance trace with --product=all so Diesel and Jet keep comparable initial-load, weekly, chart, crude, and reference timings.

recommendations.label: Watch reference payload growth
recommendations.detail: Reference payload is 148.9 KB versus 908.9 KB estimated base payload.

recommendations.label: Keep weekly rows lazy
recommendations.detail: 18,144 weekly balance rows remain outside the base workbook until requested.
